feed.title: SAP Community - Cloud Integration
feed.subtitle: Cloud Integration Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Retain Data Store in SAP CPI based on Retain Until Date
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We have a requirement for retaining the datastores data more than 90 days. Since, it is not possible as per the settings a custom Iflow is created to handle the same but it is polling all the data which is creating load on the system and might occupy more memory than required.

 

Can we add some step which can take the consideration of expiration date and extend the date based on that instead of all the data at one go. Please suggest if we can optimize this in a better way?

Thanks.

Best Regards,

Nidhi Srivastava

entry.title: Sending standard IDOCs from S4HANA to Advance Event Mesh via ASAPIO
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We have a requirement to send the IDOCs from S4HANA to AEM via ASAPIO to SAP CPI.

S4HANA (ASAPIO) ->  AEM - > SAP CPI.

Could you please share some document to achieve this requirement ? Do we need to use payload designer in this case in case of standard IDOCS ?

entry.title: Iflow HTTP Oauth authentication unsupported grant type error 400
entry.content:
hi, I created an HTTP flow with Oauth authentication but I encounter this error:

this is the iflow:

this is the security material

does anyone know what the problem could be?

entry.title: Restructure Json Payload
entry.content:
Hi All,

I am working on a an interface to query a Datalake/Databricks DB using an API, fetch the records and process them to Target system in Json Format. I have attaced 3 files for reference.

1. Sample source payload from the API Query from the DB - File "SourceDB_Payload.txt"

2. Converted the Source JSon payload to XML format - File "Json_2_xml_Source_Payload.xml"

3. Required Target JSon payload - File "Desired_Target Json_Payload.txt"

Issues am facing: 

1. Source payload has column(fieldnames) names and field values separate as shown below

{
"name": "SYSTEMSOURCECODE",
"type_text": "STRING",
"type_name": "STRING",
"position": 0
},

"EnergySystem",

whereas a normal Json or XML payload will be with the fieldname as shown below 

"SystemSourceCode": "EnergySystem"         or 

<SystemSourceCode>EnergySystem</SystemSourceCode>

2. When I converted the Source JSon to XML, all the values are under xml tag "data_array" which makes it difficult to extract and map it to the required target field.

Please advise how to proceed to this get the desired Json payload as attached.

FYI, below two fields in the target are constants.

"WellNumber": 1,
"CompletionNumber": 1,

In simple terms, My target will look like, when I extract the values under data_array in source Json and add the respective fieldnames

Thanks,

Varun

entry.title: Need .pem client certificate for CPI integration with other systems
How to connect to Databricks DB using JDBC and extract records using SAP Cloud Integration
entry.content:
Hi Team,

Please suggest how to connect to Databricks DB for JDBC connection using SAP Cloud Integration.

 I have check there is no option to upload databricks drivers under Manage Security -> JDBC Material -> JDBC Driver. I found only below Database types

1. Oracle

2. DB2

3. Microsoft SQL Server

I want to make a connection to databricks sql using JDBC connection

Sample URL:

jdbc:databricks://<hostname>.18.azuredatabricks.net:443/default;transportMode=http;ssl=1;AuthMech=3;httpPath=/sql/1.0/warehouses/12345;

Thanks,
Varun

entry.title: SAP Cloud Integration B2B Sender Process Direct
entry.content:
Hi,
I am trying to initiate Cloud Integration - B2B scenario using sender Process Direct Adapter. But interface is failing at Step 1 b - PD Lookup - Communication Sender.

Below are the header values I have mentioned in postman -

SAP_EDI_Document_Standard = SAP_S4HANA_Cloud_SOA

SAP_COM_SND_Adapter_Type = Process_Direct

TPM B2B Scenario created with sender communication as Process Direct -

Error :- 

Step 1b IFlow Error :-

I have checked my MIG ang MAP, able to simulate from the SCI payload successfully.

If I change sender adapter from Process Direct to SOAP interface is running successfully.

What step am I missing to execute it using sender process direct?

BR,

Rashmi

 

Tagging Gurus - @gunther_stuhec , @mluptak

entry.title: Context Handling for Outbound Flow in MAG
entry.content:
Hello all,
We are trying to populate data in receiver side depending upon QUALF(E1EDP19) and IDTNR(E1EDP19) field values. But all context values for IDTNR are coming in same target field which is not expected (only IDTNR values when QUALF=001 is expected)
we are maintaining below xslt mapping.

<xsl:variable name="vAmt" select="$nodes_in/QUALF"/>
<xsl:variable name="vAmy" select="$nodes_in/IDTNR"/>
<xsl:choose>
<xsl:when test="$vAmt = '013' or '001'">
<xsl:value-of select="$vAmy"/>
</xsl:when>
</xsl:choose>

 

Attached screenshot for better understanding.

 

Kindly help on this issue.

 

Regards,

Aditya

entry.title: OAuth Scopes SAP Cloud Integation
entry.content:
Hello all,

I have created a service instance for accessing Cloud Integration via API:

When I am accessing the token (e.g. via Postman) I see following scopes are granted:

If i am granting all authorizations to the process integration instance, I am getting a list of 64 scopes. I am wondering about a documentation where i can get a description of all possible scopes and which scope is for what.

Similarly, i have same challenge when i am accessing the APIM via API. Thanks for your help.

BR Helmut

entry.title: Same Counter for two nodes in target structure
entry.content:
Dear integration fellows,

I have duplicated the subtree in the target structure. So I have now on the target structure twice the node called DetaglioLinee. Because we need to map different fields from source to target. We have in the node DetaglioLinee a field called NumeroLinea. For each line item the counter increases by 1.

Now we have two subtrees with different mapping, but I want the counter to be the same. Is this possible? Can we copy maybe the occurence of the source field mapped to first subtree and increment it by then? (e.g E1EDP01 is occuring 2 times, save 2 in variable and increment by 1)

entry.title: Firefighter will support in SAP Cloud Integration?
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We have to give temporary access for the users in production.

Firefighter will support in SAP Cloud Integration?

Regards,

Naveen

entry.title: Remove double from the value of a string in multiple fields
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

I have transformed the xml to JSON and got the below output. It is possible to have a single record or multiple records, and the fields are not present in the array.

I will receive three input files. 

1st input file:

 

{
	"timestamp": "2024-03-14T00:00:00.000",
	"productID": "FG_APC_2",
	"unitOfMeasureQuantity": "1",
	"unitOfMeasure": "PC",
	"dimensionUnitOfMeasure": "IN",
	"weightUnitOfMeasure": "KG",
	"description": "Desc_FG_APC_2",
	"length": "100.000",
	"width": "12.000",
	"height": "45.000",
	"weight": "200.000",
	"identifiers": [
		{
			"label": "GTIN",
			"allowedValues": [
				{
					"value": ""
				}
			]
		}
	],
	"alternateUOM": [
		{
			"productID": "FG_APC_2",
			"unitOfMeasureQuantity": "500",
			"unitOfMeasure": "CAR",
			"length": "500.000",
			"width": "60.000",
			"height": "225.000",
			"weight": "1000.000",
			"identifiers": [
				{
					"label": "GTIN",
					"allowedValues": [
						{
							"value": ""
						}
					]
				}
			]
		},
		{
			"productID": "FG_APC_2",
			"unitOfMeasureQuantity": "5000",
			"unitOfMeasure": "PAL",
			"length": "5000.000",
			"width": "600.000",
			"height": "2250.000",
			"weight": "10000.000",
			"identifiers": [
				{
					"label": "GTIN",
					"allowedValues": [
						{
							"value": ""
						}
					]
				}
			]
		}
	],
	"messageType": "product",
	"mode": "MANAGE"
}

 

2nd input file:

 

{
    "timestamp": "2024-03-11T11:07:09-04:00",
    "productID": "LU-T05-SW",
    "unitOfMeasureQuantity": "1",
    "unitOfMeasure": "EA",
    "dimensionUnitOfMeasure": "IN",
    "weightUnitOfMeasure": "LB",
    "description": "LUMARIS TW TAPE 5M SW",
    "length": "9.0",
    "width": "6.5",
    "height": "1.25",
    "weight": "0.2",
    "identifiers": [
        {
            "label": "GTIN",
            "allowedValues": [
                {
                    "value": "0784276311007"
                }
            ]
        }
    ],
    "messageType": "product",
    "mode": "MANAGE"
}

 

3rd input file:

 

{
	"timestamp": "2024-03-14T00:00:00.000",
	"productID": "FG_APC_2",
	"unitOfMeasureQuantity": "1",
	"unitOfMeasure": "PC",
	"dimensionUnitOfMeasure": "IN",
	"weightUnitOfMeasure": "KG",
	"description": "Desc_FG_APC_2",
	"length": "100.000",
	"width": "12.000",
	"height": "45.000",
	"weight": "200.000",
	"identifiers": [
		{
			"label": "GTIN",
			"allowedValues": [
				{
					"value": ""
				}
			]
		}
	],
	"alternateUOM": [
		{
			"productID": "FG_APC_2",
			"unitOfMeasureQuantity": "500",
			"unitOfMeasure": "CAR",
			"length": "500.000",
			"width": "60.000",
			"height": "225.000",
			"weight": "1000.000",
			"identifiers": [
				{
					"label": "GTIN",
					"allowedValues": [
						{
							"value": ""
						}
					]
				}
			]
		}
	],
	"messageType": "product",
	"mode": "MANAGE"
}

 

As per my requirement, I have to remove the double quotes from the fileds UnitOfMeasureQuantity, length, width, height, and weight, value. 

alternateUOM also same from the fileds UnitOfMeasureQuantity, length, width, height, and weight, value. 

Below are the expected fields values, and the 3 input files mentioned above are also accepted at the same.

 

{
    "timestamp": "2024-03-07T07:04:49-05:00",
    "productID": "DVCL-153PH-WHC",
    "unitOfMeasureQuantity": 1,
    "unitOfMeasure": "EA",
    "dimensionUnitOfMeasure": "IN",
    "weightUnitOfMeasure": "LB",
    "description": "DIVA 150W LED 3WY",
    "length": 2.5,
    "width": 4.0,
    "height": 6.0,
    "weight": 0.27,
    "identifiers": [
        {
            "label": "GTIN",
            "allowedValues": [
                {
                    "value": "0027557003391"
                }
            ]
        }
    ],
    "alternateUOM": [
        {
            "productID": "DVCL-153PH-WHC",
            "unitOfMeasureQuantity": 3,
            "unitOfMeasure": "IP",
            "length": 4.0,
            "width": 7.5,
            "height": 6.5,
            "weight": 1.0,
            "identifiers": [
                {
                    "label": "GTIN",
                    "allowedValues": [
                        {
                            "value": "30027557003392"
                        }
                    ]
                }
            ]
        },
        {
            "productID": "DVCL-153PH-WHC",
            "unitOfMeasureQuantity": 48,
            "unitOfMeasure": "CA",
            "length": 25.5,
            "width": 15.3,
            "height": 8.9,
            "weight": 17.14,
            "identifiers": [
                {
                    "label": "GTIN",
                    "allowedValues": [
                        {
                            "value": "50027557003396"
                        }
                    ]
                }
            ]
        }
    ],
    "messageType": "product",
    "mode": "MANAGE"
}

 

I've taken alook at the blogs, but it doesn't work out for my requirement.

I require your assistance in fixing the issue urgently and archiving the requirement.

Thanks in advance.

Regards,

Thouheed

entry.title: Cloud Integration: Switch iflow Type
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

is it possible to switch the iflow type from "REST API" to "Integration Flow" ?

I do not want to build a new one from scratch. 

In my understanding, the only difference is, that REST API necessarily has to have a HTTPS Sender-Adapter.

And my requirements changed to "ProcessDirect".

Thanks in Advance.

Simon

entry.title: How to reuse scripts withing script collections
entry.content:
Hello,
As example I have following Groovy script within an iFlow:

def Message setCustomHeaderProcessToDelete(Message message) {
    return setCustomHeaderProcess(message, "Delete")
}
def Message setCustomHeaderProcessToAdd(Message message) {
    return setCustomHeaderProcess(message, "Add")
}
def Message setCustomHeaderProcessToModify(Message message) {
    return setCustomHeaderProcess(message, "Modify")
}
def Message setCustomHeaderProcess(Message message, process) {
 	def messageLog = messageLogFactory.getMessageLog(message);
    if(messageLog == null){ 
        return message; 
    } 
    messageLog.addCustomHeaderProperty("PROCESS", (String) process);
    return message 
}

I would like to move this functions to a script collection. As you can see several function call setCustomHeaderProcess.
How do I have to store setCustomHeaderProcess?
How do I have to call it from a function, by example setCustomHeaderProcessToModify?

Thanks
Bernd

entry.title: Help needed with CPI IA - MAG
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

we need to create inbound DELVRY07 IDOC in S/4 from an EDI 856 (ASN) file and for that we are using Integration Adviser - MAG. MIGs are created, and initial mapping (MAG) also done. The EDI file is having multiple HL*C (Container) & HL*O (Order) and for each HL*C we need to create sperate IDOC.

Can you suggest how can we split the HL*C with HL*O and HL*I in MAG ?

entry.title: Multiple qualifiers in MIG and context handling
entry.content:
Hello All,

 When I tried simulating my MAG I'm getting all the context values of a node as output even after assigning qualifiers (requires only first value). Can anyone help me in understanding how the context handling is done in MAGs?

Also can anyone share how the multiple qualifiers in MAG are processed while simulation?

 

Thanks,

Aditya

entry.title: OData call through HTTP Error Handling
entry.content:
Dear All,

We have a scenario as given below. 3rd party is calling an OData service synchronously through CI and Gateway and ERP as per the below flow.

3rd party --> CI --> SAP Gateway --> SAP ERP

We are not using the OData adapter due to some syntax/format issues and HTTP was chosen. Whenever any ABAP dump is thrown in the backend ERP system then the gateway forwards it to CPI but in CPI we get the error like

com.sap.it.rt.adapter.http.api.exception.HttpResponseException: An internal server error occured: HTTP operation failed invoking http://xxxxx/sap/opu/odata/sap/xxxx/xxx with statusCode: 500.
The MPL ID for the failed message is : BGYA-Z0S3gl-6qxBdCngJ8AeHb4c

The detailed error text from ABAP layer is not shown in this message. We have chosen "Return Exception to Sender" in the iFlow settings in order to pass the errors. But how to pass the error dump details from SAP service to the caller?

entry.title: SAP DMC is reaching CPI 3 times per request
entry.content:
Experts,

We've established integration between SAP Cloud Integration (CI) and SAP Digital Manufacturing Cloud (DMC) to transfer specific data from DMC to another system via CI. Within DMC, we've developed a custom process to invoke CI's API. However, we're encountering an issue where CI's API consistently triggers three times: the first two calls are empty, while the third call contains valid input data. Surprisingly, only one log entry appears in DMC's 'Monitor Production Process' section. We've configured CI's details in the destination within the SAP DMC instance and added this destination to DMC's service registry.

Please assist us in resolving this issue.

TIA,

Barath

entry.title: Authentication between SAP API Management and SAP Cloud Integration
entry.content:
Hello All,

What is the best practice to setup authentication between API Management and Cloud Integration in scenarios where APIM must trigger a CPI iFlow? 

I normally prefer using certificate based authentication by setting up a keystore in APIM and use that in the API provider that is configured for CPI. In CPI, I upload the public certificate of the keystore in the service key. However, we must purchase a certificate keypair signed by a authorised certificate authority or use a S-User generated certificate on SAP Passport. I am not aware if there is any other way to setup a key pair. Interested to know if someone can share their experience here.

I am aware that you can also use OAuth with CPI buy you must create policies inside API proxy to authenticate. Hence, I prefer the certificate based because there is no need to add additional policies in the proxy. I am curious to know how you setup this authentication in your projects?

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Process Integration test tool SAP PO usages for migration to BTP
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

As you know we have a tool called Process Integration test tool in SAP PO.

We are planning to migrate the interfaces from SAP PO to Cloud Integration capability of Integration Suite.

Does that tool can be used while regression testing of migrating interfaces from SAP PO to Cloud Integration?

May I know the replacement of such tool in Cloud Integration capability of Integration Suite?

 

Thanks,

Ramu.
